tools.name: Langfuse
tools.description: Open-source LLM engineering platform providing observability, tracing, metrics, evals, prompt management, playground, and datasets. Acquired by ClickHouse in Jan 2026.

pricing.self_hosted: Free (MIT license), infrastructure costs ~$3-4K/month at scale

tools.target_audience: Developers, ML engineers, teams building LLM applications
tools.maturity: High - 19K+ GitHub stars, YC W23, acquired by ClickHouse Jan 2026

tools.name: LangSmith
tools.description: Unified agent engineering platform from LangChain providing observability, evaluations, and prompt engineering. Framework-agnostic but optimized for LangChain/LangGraph.

pricing.notes: Per-seat pricing can be expensive for larger teams

tools.target_audience: LangChain ecosystem developers, enterprise AI teams
tools.maturity: High - backed by LangChain, widely adopted

tools.name: Braintrust
tools.description: AI observability platform for building quality AI products. Raised $80M Series B at $800M valuation in Feb 2026. Used by Notion, Zapier, Stripe, Vercel.
key_features: AI assistant 'Loop' for custom scorer generation
tools.target_audience: Production AI teams, enterprises building AI products

tools.name: Arize AI / Phoenix
tools.description: Phoenix is Arize's open-source AI observability platform for experimentation, evaluation, and troubleshooting. Built on OpenTelemetry, vendor/framework/language agnostic.
key_features: LLM evaluation with response and retrieval evals

pricing.phoenix_cloud: Free tier available, paid tiers for scale
pricing.arize_enterprise: Custom pricing for enterprise ML platform

tools.target_audience: ML engineers, data scientists, AI teams needing vendor-neutral observability
tools.maturity: High - 7,800+ GitHub stars, established enterprise platform

tools.name: AgentOps
tools.description: Agent observability platform specifically designed for AI agents. Supports OpenAI, CrewAI, Autogen, and 400+ LLMs and frameworks.

pricing.pro: $40/month - unlimited events, unlimited retention, dedicated support
pricing.enterprise: Custom - SLA, SSO, on-premise, SOC-2/HIPAA/NIST AI RMF

tools.target_audience: Teams building multi-agent systems, agent developers

tools.name: Datadog LLM Observability
tools.description: Enterprise APM platform extended with dedicated LLM observability. End-to-end tracing across AI agents with integration into existing Datadog monitoring.

pricing.notes: Auto-activates $120/day premium when detecting LLM spans. No opt-out reported. Can be used standalone without other Datadog products.

tools.target_audience: Enterprises already using Datadog, large-scale AI deployments

tools.name: Weights & Biases Weave
tools.description: Observability and evaluation platform from W&B for tracking, evaluating, and improving LLM applications. Part of the broader W&B AI developer platform.
tools.target_audience: AI researchers, ML engineers, foundation model teams
tools.maturity: High - established AI developer platform, trusted by OpenAI/Cohere/MidJourney

tools.name: Helicone
tools.description: Open-source LLM observability platform with AI Gateway capabilities. One-line proxy integration for monitoring, caching, and cost optimization.
key_features: Cost tracking across models and users
tools.target_audience: Developers needing proxy-based LLM monitoring, cost-conscious teams

tools.name: Opik (by Comet)
tools.description: Open-source LLM observability and optimization platform for debugging, evaluating, and monitoring LLM applications, RAG systems, and agentic workflows.
tools.target_audience: LLM developers, teams needing evaluation + observability
tools.maturity: Medium - backed by Comet ML, growing open-source community

tools.name: Galileo AI
tools.description: AI observability and eval engineering platform with proprietary Luna-2 foundation models for real-time guardrails and evaluation at sub-200ms latency.
key_features: RAG, agents, safety, and security evals
tools.target_audience: Teams needing real-time guardrails and evaluation at scale

tools.name: OpenLIT
tools.description: Open-source OpenTelemetry-native platform for AI Engineering covering LLM observability, GPU monitoring, guardrails, evaluations, prompt management, and more.
key_features: API key vault with remote rotation
tools.target_audience: AI engineers needing comprehensive OTEL-native observability

tools.name: OpenLLMetry (Traceloop)
tools.description: Open-source observability SDK built on OpenTelemetry for GenAI/LLM applications. Provides instrumentation that connects to 25+ observability backends.

pricing.traceloop_hosted: Free tier with 10K monthly traces, paid tiers available

tools.target_audience: Developers wanting vendor-neutral OTEL-based instrumentation

tools.name: LangWatch
tools.description: AI agent testing and LLM evaluation platform with simulation, auto-evaluation, and monitoring for pre-release and production workflows.
key_features: Token and cost monitoring across 800+ models

pricing.notes: Free tier likely available, contact for enterprise

tools.target_audience: Teams needing combined testing, evaluation, and monitoring

tools.name: Confident AI / DeepEval
tools.description: AI quality platform with DeepEval (open-source evaluation framework) and Confident AI (cloud platform for collaboration, tracing, and monitoring).
tools.target_audience: QA teams, ML engineers needing structured evaluation

tools.name: Maxim AI
tools.description: End-to-end platform unifying simulation, evaluation, and observability across the AI agent lifecycle. Enables 5x faster shipping through integrated workflows.
tools.target_audience: Enterprise teams needing full lifecycle AI observability

tools.name: Evidently AI
tools.description: Open-source tool to evaluate, test, and monitor AI-powered systems from tabular data to NLP and LLMs.
tools.target_audience: Data scientists, ML teams monitoring model quality
tools.maturity: High - established open-source ML monitoring tool

tools.name: MLflow
tools.description: Open-source AI platform with automatic tracing for agent workflows, capturing full DAG execution including parallel tool calls and conditional branches.
key_features: Full DAG capture (parallel calls, branches, loops)
tools.target_audience: ML engineers, data scientists, MLOps teams
tools.maturity: Very High - industry standard ML platform

tools.name: Splunk (AI Agent Monitoring)
tools.description: Enterprise observability platform with Q1 2026 update adding deeper insights for AI agents and digital experiences.
tools.target_audience: Large enterprises with existing Splunk deployments
tools.maturity: Very High - established enterprise monitoring vendor

tools.name: Fiddler AI
tools.description: AI Control Plane for enterprise agents providing observability and security. Positions itself as the unified control layer for AI safety and monitoring.
tools.target_audience: Enterprises needing AI governance and safety monitoring

tools.name: Guardrails AI
tools.description: Open-source framework for adding safety guardrails to LLM applications, enforcing quality and compliance policies on agent inputs/outputs in real-time.
tools.target_audience: Teams needing safety and compliance for LLM outputs

market_trends: Convergence on OpenTelemetry as the standard for agent telemetry
Shift from simple logging to evaluation-driven observability
Multi-agent system observability emerging as key differentiator
Open-source tools gaining dominance (Langfuse, Phoenix, OpenLIT)
Enterprise APM vendors (Datadog, Splunk) adding LLM-specific features
Evaluation-observability convergence (test in CI/CD, monitor in production)
Cost optimization and token analytics becoming critical
market_context.key_statistic: 51% of organizations using AI experienced at least one negative consequence from AI inaccuracy (McKinsey 2025)
market_context.notable_funding: Braintrust raised $80M Series B at $800M valuation (Feb 2026)
